title: Nuttallia lutea

It is a type of sandwort, a group of plants that are commonly found in coastal areas. The classification of Nuttallia lutea is further defined by its order Caryophyllales and class Magnoliopsida. This species of plant is native to North America and has been identified as a rare find in some regions. The classification of Nuttallia lutea provides valuable information about its characteristics and habitats.

intro: Nuttallia lutea, also known as Oregon sunshine, is a flowering plant that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety and insomnia. The bioactive constituents of Nuttallia lutea include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Nuttallia lutea is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to promote relaxation and reduce stress levels.
